titleStmt.title: SII 38.363: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 363. (A.R. No. 363 of 1912.) DĒVIKĀPURAM, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. On the north wall, first prākāra, Bṛihadāmba temple.

p.hi: King: Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Kṛishṇayadēva Mahārāya Year: Śaka 1447, Sarvasittū, (sarvajit), Chittirai.

summary.p: Records that Śīraṅganāthar, son of a village official (maṇiyakāraṉ) of Iḍaittuṟai, Irākkutapperumāḷ, had assigned several persons from Paḍaivīḍu as tiruviḷakkukuḍi for supplying six kuṟuṇi of ghee every year to the god Tirumalar-uḍaiyanayiṉār for the merit of Namaśivāya Nāyakkar, son of Mallappa nāyakkar of Marudarasarpaḍaivīdu, which was situated on the northern bank of the river Seyyāṟu, in Rājagambīraṉ-malai in Murugamaṅgalappaṟṟu in Mēlkuṉṟa-nāḍu in Palkuṉṟa-kkōṭṭam (a sub-division) of Jayaṅgoṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam.
The temple accountant, Īśura Biṟamar Rāyar Uttaṇḍi figures as signatory.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
cuvastiśrī maṉmakāmaṇṭalecura kiṣṇaiya tevamākarāyarRead tevamakārāyarpiṟutuvi rācciyam
paṇṇi Aruḷā(ṉa) niṉṟa ca(ā)kāṟttam 1000 4 100 4 10 7 mel cellā(ṉ) niṉṟa caṟuvacittu varuṣam cittirai mātam
ceyaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu palkuṉṟa koṭṭattu melkuṉṟa nāṭu murukamaṅkalapaṟṟu
rācakempiraṉmalaikkaṭaittapaṟṟu ceyyāṟṟukku vaṭakarai marutaracar paṭaiviṭṭu mallappanāyakkar
kumarar namacivāyanāyakkar Ayyaṉukku taṉmamāka Iṭaittuṟai maṇiyakāṟaṉ Irakkutapperumāḷ
makaṉ ciraṅkanātar Uṭaiyār tirumalar Uṭaiya nayiṉāṟku paṭaiviṭṭil Iṭaituṟaiyiṟukkuṭiyai
tiruviḷakku kuṭiyintu kañcaṉ koṟṟaṉ parama maṉṟāṭi co ppaḷu māran makaṉ nātaṉ māraṉ Uḷḷiṭṭār
mammaṭiyāṉ nūkkaṉ Uḷḷiṭṭār vempaṇaṉ makaṉ nūkkaṉ Uḷḷiṭṭār varuṣam Oṉṟukku Aḷakku
neyikku Inta Āṟukuṟuṇiyum varuṣamvaruṣam Aḷantu cukattileirukkavum Inta taṉmattukku
yātoruvar Akutam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkaikaraiyile kāraām pacuvai koṉṟa pāvattile poka
kaṭaivaṉ Ākavum koyilkaṇakku yicurapiṟamarrāyar Uttaṇṭi Eḻuttu §
Digital edition of SII 38.363 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
